title: AccessiSound — ESP32-S3 Sound Recognition
desc: Standalone assistive device classifying environmental sounds in real time on-device using TFLite Micro. Alerts visually impaired users via distinct vibration and audio patterns. No cloud, no phone — $17 BOM.

detail.summary: Standalone offline assistive device delivering tactile and audio notifications for doorbell, fire alarm, microwave, and phone ring. No cloud, no phone, $17 BOM.
detail.why: Visually impaired individuals often miss critical environmental audio cues. Off-the-shelf solutions require smartphones or subscriptions.
detail.problem: Real-time on-device sound classification on a microcontroller with 512 KB SRAM while fitting a TFLite Micro model alongside I2S DMA and the Arduino stack.
detail.approach: MFCC feature extraction in C++ → 13x32 feature window → quantised 2-layer CNN (TFLite Micro) → confidence threshold → unique vibration + buzzer patterns per class.

title: AI Kitchen Accessibility Assistant
desc: ESP32-CAM + MobileNet for real-time kitchen object recognition with voice command interaction and spoken feedback. Trained on university HPC.

detail.summary: Camera-based assistive system for elderly and visually impaired users that recognises kitchen objects and responds to spoken voice commands.
detail.why: Extends sound-recognition to vision. Kitchen environments are high-risk for visually impaired users.
detail.problem: Running object detection inference fast enough on ESP32-CAM's limited compute while also handling speech I/O without cloud dependency.
detail.approach: ESP32-CAM captures frames → MobileNet inference (TFLite) → speech-to-text command parsing → spoken feedback. Models trained on HPC.

title: EEG Attention Detection System
desc: ML pipeline classifying attention states from EEG brainwave data, detecting cognitive disengagement from visual stimuli. Collaborating with grad researchers.

detail.summary: Machine learning pipeline that classifies attention states from EEG brainwave data, detecting when visual stimuli cause cognitive disengagement.
detail.why: Understanding attention has broad applications from adaptive learning to human-computer interfaces.
detail.problem: EEG signals are highly noisy and person-specific. Reliable classification across subjects requires careful pipeline design.
detail.approach: EEG dataset preprocessing → frequency-band feature extraction → ML classifier (SVM, CNN, LSTM comparison) → real-time attention state output.

title: Accessible Door Entry Assistant
desc: RF-triggered assistive device enabling mobility-impaired users to activate ADA automatic door buttons remotely via a portable handheld interface.

detail.summary: RF-triggered embedded device enabling mobility-impaired users to activate ADA automatic door buttons without direct physical interaction.
detail.why: Many campus and public buildings have ADA buttons inaccessible from wheelchair height or for users with limited upper-body mobility.
detail.problem: Reliable RF triggering with enough range, low false-positive rate, and a portable form factor that can be carried or mounted.
detail.approach: Handheld transmitter with proximity detection → RF module → receiver near ADA button → relay trigger. Microcontroller-based with simple UI.

title: Methane Emission Monitoring Platform
desc: Prototype environmental data tracking system for methane emissions, developed through 12 stakeholder interviews and human-centered design at UC Berkeley.

detail.summary: Prototype environmental tracking and reporting system for methane emissions, developed using human-centered design through 12 stakeholder interviews.
detail.why: Methane is a potent greenhouse gas with limited affordable monitoring infrastructure.
detail.problem: Balancing technical sensor accuracy with accessibility for non-expert stakeholders and regulatory reporting requirements.
detail.approach: Stakeholder interview synthesis → system requirements → sensor selection → prototype data pipeline → reporting workflow design.

title: Acoustic Positioning System
desc: Full 2D acoustic positioning pipeline localizing a microphone via time-difference-of-arrival using cross-correlation, peak detection, and least-squares multilateration.

detail.summary: Full 2D acoustic positioning pipeline that localizes a microphone using time-difference-of-arrival from multiple beacon speakers.
detail.why: TDOA-based positioning is foundational for signal processing, sensor fusion, and location-aware systems.
detail.problem: Noise, reflections, and sampling jitter corrupt the cross-correlation peaks needed for accurate TDOA estimation.
detail.approach: Periodic cross-correlation → noise averaging → peak detection → least-squares multilateration. Validated on simulated and real mic recordings.

title: Voice Recognition Classifier
desc: Speaker-dependent voice command classifier using Mel-spectrogram features, PCA via SVD, and nearest-centroid classification. ~97% accuracy on real-time mic recordings.

detail.summary: Speaker-dependent voice command classifier converting speech to Mel-spectrogram features, reduced via PCA/SVD, classified with nearest-centroid.
detail.why: Understanding the full pipeline from raw audio to classification — feature engineering, dimensionality reduction, and decision boundaries.
detail.problem: Real-time classification must be robust to mic position, room acoustics, and speaker variation.
detail.approach: Audio → Mel spectrogram → alignment + normalisation → PCA via SVD → nearest-centroid classifier in PCA space.

title: Multi-Robot Task Allocation Framework
desc: Disaster-response coordination framework for multi-agent robots using cvxpy optimization and ROS. 70% efficiency improvement; validated on TACC supercomputing cluster.

detail.summary: Disaster-response coordination framework for multi-agent robots using convex optimization and ROS, validated on TACC supercomputing infrastructure.
detail.why: Efficient multi-robot coordination is critical for search-and-rescue where robot teams need to cover large areas with minimal overlap.
detail.problem: Scaling path-planning optimization to many robots within real-time constraints — traditional approaches become intractable at scale.
detail.approach: cvxpy-based optimization → ROS integration → Gazebo simulation → TACC HPC cluster benchmarking. 70% efficiency gain, 45% faster planning.
detail.status: Complete — presented at SCCUR and STARS 2024
